subscores.label: Did IDs / sources check out?
subscores.plain: Citations used real CV and portfolio IDs — no invented hashes.

subscores.label: Were claims true vs JD + CV?
subscores.plain: Azure products and HTML/CSS handled carefully; a few soft over-matches.

subscores.label: Were the 0–100 scores fair?
subscores.plain: All four today-CV scores sit inside the human fair range.

subscores.label: Were project swaps sensible?
subscores.plain: No fake portfolio boost on the current score; one weak secondary swap.

jobs.title: Job 1 — AI Engineer (Azure / Riyadh)
jobs.what_went_well: Correctly said Azure AI Foundry and Azure OpenAI are not on the CV. Only general Azure is partial. Swapping the game aimbot project for JobPilot (+6) makes sense.
jobs.weak_spot: Some evidence quotes are shortened with “…”, so they are harder to verify word-for-word.

jobs.title: Job 2 — AI-Assisted Web Developer
jobs.what_went_well: HTML/CSS correctly treated as only partial (JS/React yes, HTML/CSS not explicit). Tenure used the real date fact (~16 months). Remote matched.
jobs.weak_spot: Suggested jump from 68→78 (+10) is a bit high. The second swap (Jarvis → WhatsApp) adds little because AI-tool skills were already matched.

jobs.title: Job 3 — Junior AI Engineer (Lahore, onsite)
jobs.what_went_well: Kept all four CV projects (no unnecessary swaps). Tenure and degree matched. Honestly marked Lahore onsite as not shown on the CV.
jobs.weak_spot: Soft skills are a little strict, but that is acceptable.

jobs.title: Job 4 — AI/ML & Generative AI Engineer
jobs.what_went_well: Big recovery vs the old broken run that scored this job 15. LangGraph, RAG, and PyTorch are grounded. Bedrock stays partial (listed, not proven in a project).
jobs.weak_spot: Calling NLP fully “matched” is a stretch (voice/RAG is related, not classic NLP). Swapping Linnworks for JobPilot is okay but drops a strong AWS-titled project.

prior_runs.assessment: Too high — weak evidence rules, inflated fit
Too low — valid CV evidence thrown away (Job4 scored 15)
Balanced — all four scores inside human fair ranges
